text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** is printing money right now. **$73.42 top scale** across 23 calls needing 117 hands. Mid City, TSG, and ESI are all running strong; ESI's Cologix data center gig pays **double-time OT** on anything past the 40-hour floor. This is the gold standard for inside work in the Midwest—don't sleep on it.

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** has **$54.75 scale** and 43 calls chasing 187 bodies. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center jobs are **10% over scale with rollups**, running 5-12s Monday through Friday with weekend work. That's solid money stacked on top of solid hours—the kind of consistency that pays down debt fast.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** is a data center play with teeth. Four contractors running Port Washington (MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein) are all stacking **journeyman wage plus $10/hour base**. That's premium pay for what's essentially steady work, 41 calls deep.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** offers **$44.80 scale plus $100-$150 daily incentive pay** (nontaxed) at the Polaris Forge Data Center. Parsons Electric is burning through 75 calls total across multiple shifts. Per diem swallows the modest hourly rate—100+ mile radius, which means commitment, but the math works for serious travelers.

**LU-369 Louisville, KY** is buzzing with automotive shutdown and NMA project work. Toyota, Ford LAP, and various shops are spreading 13 calls across 124 hands—lighter board but tight spreads and guaranteed hours.

Data centers remain the economic engine driving this entire market.
